codeReview.summary: The reviewed change does not implement the feature description. It adds parity tests only, leaves the persisted corpus file untouched, and the committed tests assert stale behavior on the default/local runtime instead of verifying corpus enrichment end-to-end.

issues.description: The committed characterization test expects `current_price`, `market_cap`, and `last_updated` to be null for the checked-in `./data/opengecko.db` runtime, which contradicts the feature requirement to enrich the persisted corpus so `/coins/markets` core fields are populated for sampled parity assets.

The only committed coin-detail test exercises the validation bootstrap `:memory:` path on port 3102 rather than directly verifying the checked-in persisted corpus at `data/opengecko.db`, so it does not prove the owned behavior that the persisted corpus itself contains the required `/coins/{id}.market_data` inputs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-provider-worker procedure is a poor fit for this data-only corpus-enrichment feature: the worker followed it toward shared-runtime investigation and ended up shipping tests without the required corpus change.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback reports deviations because the worker focused on characterization/runtime exposure instead of completing the corpus update; the feature description in mission features.json explicitly says not to spend this feature on runtime seeding-boundary changes.

summary: Fail: commit d75fdee only adds tests and does not deliver the persisted corpus enrichment required by the feature. One committed test encodes the old null-field behavior for `data/opengecko.db`, and the other verifies only the validation bootstrap path rather than the checked-in corpus itself.
